text: When setting project goals, it’s important to define them clearly. One effective method is using SMART goals, which means goals are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. This helps your team know exactly what to aim for. Break the project into smaller milestones, which are checkpoints along the way to track progress. Each milestone should lead to a deliverable, such as a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by communicating with everyone involved—like clients or team members—so expectations match. This reduces confusion and keeps the project on the right track.
